vulnerabilities.description: User-controlled `q` parameter from request.args is interpolated into a SQL string via f-string and executed without parameterized binding.
User-controlled `filename` from JSON body is interpolated into a shell command run with shell=True.
User-controlled `filename` is joined to UPLOAD_DIR with no sanitization; `../` escapes the upload root.
User-controlled `msg` is interpolated into a template string and rendered by render_template_string, allowing Jinja2 expression execution.
login_required only checks session existence; the handler returns arbitrary user profiles without verifying ownership.
Flask secret_key and a live-looking API key are embedded in source.
User-controlled URL is fetched with requests.get and no allowlist or IP filtering, allowing access to internal services.

false_positive_traps.description: Uses parameterized binding via `?` placeholder. Safe despite being in the same file as VULN-001.
pickle.loads on a server-controlled cache file, not user input.
render_template_string used with a constant template and the user input bound as a Jinja2 context variable (auto-escaped).
eval() on a hardcoded constant — no user input reaches the sink.
Redirect target chosen from a hardcoded allowlist; user input is only used as a dictionary key.
subprocess.run with a hardcoded argv list and no user input.
requests.get on a hardcoded URL; no SSRF surface.
os.system with a hardcoded string literal.
User-controlled filename is sanitized with werkzeug.secure_filename before joining to UPLOAD_DIR.
Profile lookup keyed by session-derived user id, not URL parameter.
